session.noCurrentStory: Add or select a story to estimate.

home.headline: Planning Poker
home.subheadline: Simultaneous voting, instant reveal. No anchoring bias.
home.team_note: Team mode requires Firebase. Configure secrets to enable.
home.why_body: Planning Poker prevents anchoring bias — the tendency for the first number mentioned to dominate discussion. By voting simultaneously and revealing all at once, everyone's independent estimate is surfaced, leading to richer discussion and better estimates.

learn.title: About Planning Poker
learn.fibonacci_body: Fibonacci numbers (1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13...) reflect the increasing uncertainty of larger estimates. The gaps between large numbers force the team to acknowledge uncertainty rather than pretending 14 and 15 are meaningfully different.
learn.wideband_body: Planning Poker is based on the Wideband Delphi method: gather independent estimates, reveal simultaneously, discuss divergence, re-estimate. The discussion between high and low voters surfaces hidden assumptions and risks.
learn.tip1: If votes are close (e.g., 5 and 8), just pick the majority and move on.
learn.tip2: If votes diverge widely, ask the lowest voter and highest voter to explain first.
learn.tip3: ? means 'I don't understand the story enough to estimate' — clarify before re-voting.
learn.tip4: ☕ means 'I need a break' — honor it.
learn.tip5: Velocity is what matters, not the absolute size of story points.
